title: 2010_aerodyanmiocs_interaction_in_lean_module_combustors
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 2]
Here's the extracted technical content:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   m = integral(rho*U*dA) = rho*U*A
*   P_avg = (1/m) * integral(P*dm) = (1/(rho*U*A)) * integral(P*rho*U*dA)
*   lambda_1-2 = (P_1 - P_2) / (P_1 - p_1)
*   C_p1-2 = (p_2 - p_1) / (P_1 - p_1)

**2. Graphs/charts:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig. 1 Test facility:**
    *   Inlet Louvres
    *   Inlet Plenum
    *   Air Flow
    *   Inlet flare and honeycomb
    *   Test Cell
    *   Working Section
    *   Prop shaft
    *   Test Cell Floor Vertical Support
    *   Motor
    *   Plinth
    *   Exit Louvres
    *   Air Flow
    *   Fan Room
    *   Centrifugal Fan
    *   Sub-Floor Plenum

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, and Parameters:**

*   OGV row: 160 vanes
*   Prediffuser area ratio: 1.8
*   Nondimensional length: 2.23
*   Flow coefficient (Va/U): phi = 0.403
*   Mass flow: approximately 4 kg s^-1 i
*   Axial velocity: approximately 40 m s^-1
*   Mach number: 0.12
*   OGV Reynolds number: approximately 1.6 x 10^5
*   20 "fuel injectors" represented by 20 simple holes. initially set to take 30% of compressor discharge flow.
*   Mass flow balance: +/- 1%
*   mass-weighted total pressure: +/- 1 mm H2O,
*   mass-weighted total pressure: less than +/- 0.5% of the dynamic pressure at OGV exit.
*   Repeatability: +/- 0.005
*   Rig inlet (X1), rotor exit (X2), OGV exit (X3), prediffuser exit (X4), within the inner (X5i), and outer (X50) feed annuli and just upstream of the dummy injector (XI)

text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical equations:**

*   (Px2 - Plocal) / (Px2 - Px2)

**2. Graphs/charts:**

*   **Fig. 5 Rig inlet axial velocity profile (X1)**
    *   **Axes:**
        *   y-axis: U/U
        *   x-axis: (r-ri)/(ro-ri)
    *   **Curves:**
        *   minj = 30%
        *   minj = 50%
        *   minj = 70%
    *   **Key values:** The graph shows the axial velocity profile for different injection rates, reaching a maximum velocity of approximately 1.05 U/U between (r-ri)/(ro-ri) = 0.2 and 0.8 for all three curves.

**3. Tables:**

*   There are no tables in the extract.

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig. 4 Cowl inlet data:**
    *   **Components:**
        *   Diagrams are pressure profiles with the injection rate labeled. Minj = 50% (top) and minj = 70% (bottom).
    *   **Labels:**
        *   Height = 80 mm
        *   Sector angle Δθ = 9 deg (4-OGV passages))

**5. Key numbers, constants, parameters:**

*   minj = 30%, 50%, 70% (Cowl flow injection rates)
*   Height of cowl inlet data = 80 mm
*   Sector angle Δθ = 9 deg (4-OGV passages)
*   5% of the rotor total pressure rise (equivalent to cowl flow suction field)
*   ±5% variation in mass flow per sector (between inline and in between injectors)

title: 2010_aerodyanmiocs_interaction_in_lean_module_combustors
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 7]
Here's the technical content extracted from the gas turbine research page:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Rn = ((r - ri)/(ro - ri))
*   phi = (Va/U)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   Figure 10: Axial velocity contours at prediffuser exit (X4)
    *   X-axis: U/U from 0.0 to 1.5 (incrementing by 0.1)
    *   Curves: Represent axial velocity contours at prediffuser exit
        *   Figure 10(a): minj = 30%
        *   Figure 10(b): minj = 50%
        *   Figure 10(c): minj = 70%
        *   height = 65.9 mm, sector angle = 18 deg (8-OGV passages)

**3. Tables:**

*   Table 3: Inner annulus performance data
    *   Headers: Test No., lambda(X2-X5i), lambda(X3-X5i), Cp(X2-X5i), Cp(X3-X5i)
    *   Data:
        *   Test No. 1: 0.224, 0.159, 0.71, 0.71
        *   Test No. 2: 0.232, 0.204, 0.72, 0.69
        *   Test No. 3: 0.262, 0.284, 0.71, 0.65

**4. Diagrams:**

*   No explicit diagrams were provided, but Figure 10 shows axial velocity contours

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   OGV total pressure loss coefficient varied from 0.097 to 0.153
*   Overall sector average OGV pressure loss coefficient: 0.129
*   Diffuser height: 65.9 mm
*   Sector Angle: 18 degrees

**Nomenclature:**

*   A = Passage Area
*   AR = Area Ratio
*   c = Outlet guide vane chord
*   Cp = Static pressure rise coefficient
*   h = Annulus passage height
*   L = Diffuser length
*   m = Mass flow rate
*   N = Rotor speed
*   P, p = Local total or static pressure
*   R = Gas constant (perfect gas law)
*   Rn = Nondimensional radius ((r - ri)/(ro - ri))
*   r = Radius relative to rig centerline
*   ri, ro = Inner casing, outer casing radius
*   s = Blade pitch
*   T,t = Total or static temperature
*   U = Rotor midpassage blade speed
*   Va = Midpassage axial velocity
*   x = Axial distance
*   alpha = kinetic energy flux coefficient
*   phi = flow coefficient (Va/U)
*   rho = density
*   lambda = total pressure loss coefficient

**Subscripts:**
*   X1 = rotor inlet traverse plane
*   X2 = rotor exit/OGV inlet traverse plane
*   X3 = OGV exit traverse plane
*   X4 = prediffuser exit traverse plane
*   X5i = inner annulus traverse plane
*   X5o = outer annulus traverse plane

**Superscripts:**
*   *Area Weighted Spatially Averaged Mean Value*
*   ~ = *Mass Weighted Spatially Averaged Mean Value*
